notes: Merged and deduplicated from v4. Duplicates removed: NIH RePORTER (paused, subsumed by NIH Open FOAs), NSF Award Search (paused, subsumed by NSF Open Solicitations), Horizon Europe/CORDIS (paused, subsumed by EU Funding & Tenders Portal), EIC (paused, subsumed by EU Funding & Tenders Portal), EU Funding & Tenders Horizon Programmes (paused, duplicate), ESIF/Cohesion (paused, separate scraper needed), EU Funding Portal aggregator eufundingportal.eu (paused, no scraper), UKRI Active Opportunities (duplicate of UKRI Funding Opportunities), d-portal (subsumed by IATI Datastore), Wellcome Open Research (subsumed by Wellcome Trust main entry), Robert Bosch Stiftung Arts entry (merged into main Robert Bosch Stiftung EU entry), Nuffield Foundation duplicate (merged), Leverhulme Trust duplicate (merged), Alfred P Sloan Foundation duplicate (merged), Esmee Fairbairn Foundation duplicate (merged), Pew Charitable Trusts duplicate (merged), Joyce Foundation duplicate (merged), Volkswagen Stiftung duplicate (merged), Schmidt Futures duplicate (merged into Schmidt Sciences entry), Wellcome Leap duplicate (merged), Wellcome Trust Open Calls duplicate (merged). gcgh.grandchallenges.org/grant-opportunities added as dedicated scraper target. All new sources from gap analysis added. v6: NIHR entries now use paginate=true/max_pages=8 to capture all funding pages; Fulbright link_filter=/program. v7: bulk scraper_config upgrade — depth=0→1 for 23 sources, pagination added to 261 sources, link_filter added to 53 sources, use_playwright enforced on 0 sources.

sources.name: Grants.gov (Simpler Grants)
sources.notes: RESTful API; key obtained from account settings on simpler.grants.gov

sources.name: NIH Open FOAs (Grants.gov)

scraper_config.query: health artificial intelligence digital clinical research

sources.notes: Open NIH funding opportunities via Grants.gov Simpler API. Filters to posted/forecasted FOAs only — not RePORTER award records.

sources.name: NSF Open Solicitations (Grants.gov)

scraper_config.query: artificial intelligence health digital machine learning

sources.notes: Open NSF solicitations via Grants.gov Simpler API. Filters to posted/forecasted opportunities only — not NSF Award Search records.

sources.name: SAM.gov – Contract & Grant Opportunities
sources.notes: Public access: 10 req/day; registered entities: 1,000/day

sources.name: SBIR.gov – Small Business Innovation Research
sources.notes: Topic API + Award API; bulk downloads in XLS/JSON/XML. Award data 290 MB full file.

sources.name: USASpending.gov
sources.notes: Comprehensive open API; free; no key needed.

sources.name: Federal Audit Clearinghouse (GSA)
sources.notes: Launched Oct 2023 by GSA, replacing old facdissem.census.gov. Open API; relates to grant compliance not opportunity discovery.

sources.name: ARPA-H – Open Funding Opportunities
sources.notes: Advanced Research Projects Agency for Health (ARPA-H); ISOs, programs, SBIR/STTR, and initiative opportunities listed on rolling basis.

sources.name: DARPA – R&D Opportunities (BAAs)
sources.notes: Primary vehicle is Broad Agency Announcements (BAAs); posted to SAM.gov and DARPA site. Primarily contracts/cooperative agreements/OTs rather than traditional grants.

sources.name: PCORI – Patient-Centered Outcomes Research Institute
sources.notes: Independent non-profit authorized by Congress; funds CER and D&I research. Three cycles per year; up to $120M per Broad Pragmatic Studies PFA cycle. No API; scraping required.

sources.name: BARDA – Biomedical Advanced Research & Development Authority
sources.notes: HHS agency; ~$1–2B/yr for medical countermeasure development. BARDA DRIVe programme actively funds AI diagnostics. Opportunities posted to SAM.gov and grants.gov.

sources.name: USDA NIFA – National Institute of Food and Agriculture
sources.notes: Applications via Grants.gov. Funds competitive, capacity, and noncompetitive grants for research, education, and extension.

sources.name: DOE Office of Science – PAMS Grant Portal
sources.notes: Uses PAMS for applications; FOAs posted on Grants.gov and science.osti.gov.

sources.name: DOE ARPA-E – Advanced Research Projects Agency-Energy (eXCHANGE)
sources.notes: ARPA-E eXCHANGE is the mandatory submission portal. ~$400M annual budget; awards $500K–$10M over 2–3 years. [audit 2026-07-22] timeout during probe

sources.name: NEH – National Endowment for the Humanities
sources.notes: Funded Projects Query Form at apps.neh.gov/publicquery provides abstracts back to 1980. No dedicated REST API.

sources.name: NEA – National Endowment for the Arts
sources.notes: Only federal arts funder serving all 50 states. FY 2026 Grants for Arts Projects active.

sources.name: IMLS – Institute of Museum and Library Services
sources.notes: Award abstracts back to 1997 at imls.gov/grants. Free public API for award data: api.imls.gov. [audit 2026-07-22] timeout during probe

sources.name: NASA – NSPIRES (Research Opportunities in Space and Earth Science)
sources.notes: NASA's official research solicitation system. ROSES-26 releases July 2026. [audit 2026-07-22] auto-paused: conn_error

sources.name: DOD – ONR / ARO / AFOSR (Defense Research BAAs)
sources.notes: Three main DoD basic research offices: ONR, ARO, AFOSR. FY2026 MURI ~$170M total.

sources.name: DOD CDMRP – Congressionally Directed Medical Research Programs
sources.notes: ~$1B+/yr in disease-specific programmes including Breast Cancer, Prostate, Neurofibromatosis, TB, Peer Reviewed Medical Research. Applications via Grants.gov. [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://cdmrp.health.mil/funding/default.shtml → site root

sources.name: AHRQ – Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality
sources.notes: HHS agency with ~$380M annual budget. Funds health services research, patient safety, and healthcare quality improvement.

sources.name: CDC – Centers for Disease Control and Prevention Grants
sources.notes: ~$8–9B in grants annually. Opportunities posted on Grants.gov.

sources.name: HRSA – Health Resources and Services Administration
sources.notes: HHS agency with ~$12.2B in FY2024 grants. Focus: community health centers, rural health, health workforce.

sources.name: SAMHSA – Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration
sources.notes: HHS agency with ~$6–7B annual grants. SAMHSA Grants Dashboard lists open, current, and past grants.

sources.name: NICHD – National Institute of Child Health & Human Development
sources.notes: NIH institute with active maternal/neonatal AI FOAs; highly relevant for MAM*AI. Applications via eRA Commons / Grants.gov.

sources.name: NLM – National Library of Medicine
sources.notes: Biomedical informatics training and research grants; highly aligned with Computational Biology and Clinical AI. G08, R01, R21 mechanisms. [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://www.nlm.nih.gov/ep/Grants.html → site root

sources.name: NCATS – National Center for Advancing Translational Sciences
sources.notes: NIH translational science centre; CTSA network, TRND, NCATS ASPIRE programmes. AI translation focus for clinical deployment. [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://ncats.nih.gov/funding/opportunities → site root

sources.name: NCI – National Cancer Institute (standalone FOAs)
sources.notes: Largest NIH institute by budget (~$7B). Dedicated cancer AI/ML FOAs including ITCR (Informatics Technology for Cancer Research) programme.

sources.name: NIMH – National Institute of Mental Health
sources.notes: Significant AI in mental health FOAs; separate tracking from general NIH Grants.gov feed recommended. [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://www.nimh.nih.gov/funding/grants-and-contracts → site root

sources.name: ONC – Office of the National Coordinator for Health IT
sources.notes: HHS ONC; funds clinical AI interoperability, EHR AI research, and health information exchange. [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://www.healthit.gov/topic/research-evaluation/grant-funding → site root

sources.name: VA – Veterans Affairs Merit Review Program
sources.notes: Large federal funder for clinical AI in veteran health; separate from NIH. Open to VA-affiliated investigators.

sources.name: US National AI Research Resource (NAIRR)
sources.notes: Pilot compute + data access programme for AI research. Not traditional grants but essential infrastructure access for AI researchers.

sources.name: Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation
sources.notes: No public API. Download the spreadsheet from the committed grants page. Also accessible via Candid/ProPublica.

sources.name: Grand Challenges Global (BMGF) – Grant Opportunities
sources.notes: Canonical BMGF Grand Challenges open call listing at gcgh.grandchallenges.org/grant-opportunities. Active calls include Grand Challenges South Africa (cost-disruptive diagnostics), Grand Challenges India, and global diarrheal disease burden estimation. Separate from grandchallenges.org which covers the broader partnership network. Scrape this URL directly for open calls.

sources.name: Grand Challenges Explorations (BMGF / Grand Challenges Canada / USAID)
sources.notes: Award database — past grants. Separate from /grant-opportunities which lists open calls. Also see grandchallenges.org for broader partner network map.

sources.name: Wellcome Trust
sources.notes: One of the largest biomedical funders globally — £16B endowment. Includes Early Career Awards, Investigator Awards, Discovery Research, Data for Science and Health stream, and Africa Programmes. Data accessible via 360Giving GrantNav API & Datastore.

sources.name: Wellcome Data for Science and Health
sources.notes: Dedicated Wellcome stream for data science + health intersections. Separate call cycle from main Wellcome research schemes. [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://wellcome.org/grant-funding/schemes/data-science-and-health-challenge-grant → site root

sources.name: Wellcome Africa Research Programmes
sources.notes: Direct Wellcome grants for African institutions including WACCBIP, ACEGID, ACACIAS programmes. Separate call cycle from main Wellcome schemes. Highly relevant for Rwanda AI Lab and LiGHT Lab partnerships. [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://wellcome.org/grant-funding/schemes/african-research-development → site root

sources.name: Wellcome Leap – Research Challenges
sources.notes: Wellcome's ARPA-style research challenge programmes (health moonshots). Small number of active calls; monitor directly. Separate from Wellcome Trust main grant schemes.

sources.name: Ford Foundation
sources.notes: No API; scraping required. Also available via Candid FDO and ProPublica 990.

sources.name: MacArthur Foundation
sources.notes: No API; scraping required. MacArthur Fellows ('genius grant') — $800K over 5 years. Available via Candid FDO.

sources.name: Open Society Foundations
sources.notes: Soros-backed foundation — major international grants. No API; scraping required. Also accessible via Candid.

sources.name: Robert Wood Johnson Foundation (RWJF)
sources.notes: No API; scraping required. Actively funding Health & Equity AI programs including Pioneering Ideas; current active FOAs specifically on clinical AI fairness. Also in Candid FDO.

sources.name: Rockefeller Foundation
sources.notes: Active grants search page. Pagination via WordPress paged=N or /page/N/.

sources.name: Bloomberg Philanthropies
sources.notes: No API; scraping required. Some data via ProPublica 990.

sources.name: Simons Foundation
sources.notes: No API; scraping required. [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://www.simonsfoundation.org/grants/ → site root

sources.name: Alfred P. Sloan Foundation
sources.notes: No API; scraping required. Available via Candid FDO.

sources.name: Open Philanthropy
sources.notes: No API; structured HTML — relatively easy to scrape. [audit 2026-07-22] redirects to https://coefficientgiving.org/funds/ — verify new URL

sources.name: Chan Zuckerberg Initiative
sources.notes: No API; scraping required.

sources.name: Andrew W. Mellon Foundation
sources.notes: Largest funder of humanities in US. No API; scraping required.

sources.name: Kresge Foundation
sources.notes: No API; scraping required. [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://kresge.org/grants-and-loans/ → site root

sources.name: Lumina Foundation
sources.notes: No API; scraping required.

sources.name: Gordon and Betty Moore Foundation
sources.notes: $11.4B endowment; distributed $401.8M in 2024 across 952 grants. Focus areas: Environmental Conservation, Science, Bay Area. Also in Candid FDO.

sources.name: W.K. Kellogg Foundation
sources.notes: $8.2B endowment; focuses on children, families, communities, and racial equity. Also accessible via Candid FDO and ProPublica 990.

sources.name: William & Flora Hewlett Foundation
sources.notes: No public API; scraping required. Awarded >$450M in grants. Also in Candid FDO.

sources.name: Pew Charitable Trusts
sources.notes: No public API; scraping required. Does not typically accept unsolicited applications; proactive grantmaking model. Also in Candid FDO. [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://www.pewtrusts.org/en/grants → site root

sources.name: Carnegie Corporation of New York
sources.notes: $4.1B endowment. Does not typically accept unsolicited proposals. Also accessible via Candid FDO and ProPublica 990.

sources.name: Howard Hughes Medical Institute (HHMI)
sources.notes: $27.1B endowment. Announced $245M for AI-driven life sciences projects. Also in Candid FDO.

sources.name: Burroughs Wellcome Fund
sources.notes: Focuses on early-career biomedical scientists and underfunded areas of science. Also in Candid FDO.

sources.name: Children's Investment Fund Foundation (CIFF)
sources.notes: Disbursed $468M in 2021. Does not solicit unsolicited proposals. Also in Candid FDO.

sources.name: Mastercard Foundation
sources.notes: Headquartered in Toronto. Focuses on young people in Africa and Indigenous youth in Canada. Does not typically accept unsolicited proposals. Also in Candid FDO. [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://mastercardfdn.org/all-programs/ → site root

sources.name: David and Lucile Packard Foundation
sources.notes: Major US private foundation. Proactive grantmaking; does not generally accept unsolicited proposals. Also accessible via Candid FDO and ProPublica 990.

sources.name: Joyce Foundation
sources.notes: Chicago-based; focuses on the Great Lakes region. Does not accept unsolicited proposals for most programs. Also accessible via Candid FDO and ProPublica 990.

sources.name: John S. and James L. Knight Foundation
sources.notes: Invitation-only model for most programs; some open challenge competitions. Also accessible via Candid FDO. [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://knightfoundation.org/grants/ → site root

sources.name: Spencer Foundation
sources.notes: Invests in education research. Open LOI/application cycles published on website. Also accessible via Candid FDO.

sources.name: Ewing Marion Kauffman Foundation
sources.notes: Focus: equitable economic mobility through entrepreneurship and education. Also accessible via Candid FDO and ProPublica 990.

sources.name: William T. Grant Foundation
sources.notes: Reduces inequality in youth outcomes and improves use of research evidence in policy. Active 2026: William T. Grant Scholars ($425K), Institutional Challenge Grant (up to $650K). Also accessible via Candid FDO.

sources.name: Conrad N. Hilton Foundation
sources.notes: ~$7.36B in assets. Does not typically accept unsolicited proposals. Also accessible via Candid FDO and ProPublica 990.

sources.name: Doris Duke Foundation
sources.notes: Focus: arts, environmental conservation, medical research, and child well-being. Also accessible via Candid FDO and ProPublica 990.

sources.name: Annie E. Casey Foundation
sources.notes: Major US foundation focused on vulnerable children, youth, and families. Proactive grantmaking. Also accessible via Candid FDO and ProPublica 990.

sources.name: Arnold Ventures
sources.notes: $2B+ foundation actively funding health systems reform, clinical AI, and research transparency. No unsolicited applications but actively announces open calls. Monitor closely. [audit 2026-07-22] auto-paused: conn_error

sources.name: EU Funding & Tenders Portal (Horizon, LIFE, etc.)
sources.notes: Canonical EC SEDIA API source (apiKey=SEDIA). Fetches all open/forthcoming calls across Horizon, ERC, EIC, LIFE, Digital Europe and more.

sources.name: European Research Council (ERC)
sources.notes: ERC calls also accessible through the main EU Funding & Tenders Portal API. [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://erc.europa.eu/funding/calls-for-proposals → site root

sources.name: Horizon Europe Cluster 1: Health (dedicated call tracking)
sources.notes: Dedicated Cluster 1 Health calls within Horizon Europe — worth tracking specifically in addition to the general SEDIA feed to catch health-specific calls early.

sources.name: IHI – Innovative Health Initiative (formerly IMI)
sources.notes: EC–EFPIA public-private partnership (rebranded from IMI2 to IHI). Major AI diagnostics, real-world evidence, and digital health calls. Budget Horizon Europe 2021–2027 €2.4B. Separate submission process from main Horizon.

sources.name: DFG – Deutsche Forschungsgemeinschaft (German Research Foundation)
sources.notes: GEPRIS database (gepris.dfg.de) covers all DFG-funded projects since early 2000s. REST API available for GEPRIS. Major bilateral agreements with CIHR, NSERC, SSHRC and others.

sources.name: Swiss National Science Foundation (SNSF)
sources.notes: Applications via the SNSF Portal (portal.snf.ch). P3 database for funded projects. Active 2026 joint call with NHMRC Australia for AI in healthcare research. Also partners with Tri-Agency Canada and DFG Germany. [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://www.snf.ch/en/funding/ → site root

sources.name: ANR – Agence Nationale de la Recherche (France)
sources.notes: France's national research funding agency. Active bilateral programs with NSERC, DFG, NWO and others. Part of FLAG-ERA consortium. [audit 2026-07-22] redirects to https://anr.fr/en/ — verify new URL

sources.name: ANRS – Maladies infectieuses émergentes (France)
sources.notes: French infectious disease and global health research agency (merger of ANRS and REACTing). Active calls for global health and Africa-focused research. Highly relevant for ULTR-AI TB work. [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://www.anrs.fr/en/calls-for-projects → site root

sources.name: NWO – Dutch Research Council (Netherlands)
sources.notes: National research council of the Netherlands. Includes Dutch Research Agenda (NWA) programs. No bulk API; scraping required.

sources.name: Swedish Research Council (Vetenskapsrådet)
sources.notes: Sweden's largest governmental research funding body. Participates in NordForsk Nordic cooperation. No public REST API.

sources.name: Research Council of Norway (Norges Forskningsråd)
sources.notes: Allocates NOK 10Bn annually. Participates in NordForsk Nordic cooperation. No public bulk API.

sources.name: NordForsk – Nordic Research and Innovation Funding
sources.notes: Organisation under Nordic Council of Ministers. Funding partners: Denmark, Finland, Iceland, Norway, Sweden research councils. No public REST API.

sources.name: FWF – Austrian Science Fund
sources.notes: Austria's primary research funding agency; DFG partner. Part of Open Research Europe consortium. [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://www.fwf.ac.at/en/research-funding/funding-opportunities → site root

sources.name: FCT – Fundação para a Ciência e a Tecnologia (Portugal)
sources.notes: Portugal's main funding agency. Part of Open Research Europe consortium. No public REST API. [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://www.fct.pt/en/financing/ → site root

sources.name: Academy of Finland / Research Council of Finland
sources.notes: Finland's primary research funding body. Participates in NordForsk. No public REST API.

sources.name: Novo Nordisk Foundation
sources.notes: As of Feb 2026 the wealthiest charitable foundation in the world (~$220B net worth). Awarded US$1.83B in grants in 2024. Applications via 'NORMA' online system.

sources.name: Lundbeck Foundation (Denmark)
sources.notes: Major Danish biomedical funder with focus on brain research and AI. Often co-funds with Novo Nordisk Foundation. Accessible also via Research Portal Denmark (Forskningsportal.dk). [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://lundbeckfonden.com/en/grants → site root

sources.name: Research Portal Denmark (Forskningsportal.dk)
sources.notes: Consolidated Danish grant database aggregating Carlsberg Foundation, Independent Research Fund Denmark (DFF), Lundbeck Foundation, Novo Nordisk Foundation, VELUX FOUNDATION, and Villum Foundation. No public REST API; scraping feasible.

sources.name: VolkswagenStiftung (Volkswagen Foundation)
sources.notes: Major German private science foundation. Active 2026: Lichtenberg Endowed Professorships, Opus Magnum, Challenges for Europe. No public REST API.

sources.name: Robert Bosch Stiftung (Robert Bosch Foundation)
sources.notes: One of the major foundations in Europe. Supports education, health, social affairs, and civic engagement. Continuous application process via central online portal. No public REST API.

sources.name: European Climate Foundation
sources.notes: Major philanthropic initiative working with 700+ partners on climate action in Europe and globally. Proactive partnership model; monitor for open RFPs. No public REST API.

sources.name: Culture Moves Europe – Mobility Grants
sources.notes: EU mobility grants for artists and culture professionals. [audit 2026-07-22] auto-paused: dns_fail

sources.name: Erasmus+ – EU Education & Youth Grants
sources.notes: EU's flagship education/training/youth/sport program.

sources.name: MSCA Marie Curie – Individual Fellowships
sources.notes: EU research fellowship program for early and experienced researchers.

sources.name: Italian National Recovery Plan (PNRR) – Health Extended Partnerships
sources.notes: €20B+ Italian NRRP; health AI innovation funded under Extended Partnership calls (PE8, PE9). Relevant for LiGHT Lab EPFL collaborations. [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://www.governo.it/it/pnrr/ → site root

sources.name: UKRI Gateway to Research (GtR)
sources.notes: Free, open API; data available via 3 API endpoints. Also bulk CSV download.

sources.name: UKRI Funding Opportunities
sources.notes: No API for live calls; scraping required. Awarded grants accessible via GtR API.

sources.name: NIHR – National Institute for Health & Care Research
sources.notes: Largest funder of clinical and applied health research in the UK; annual budget >£1B. Major programmes: Research for Patient Benefit (RfPB), Health Technology Assessment (HTA), Efficacy & Mechanism Evaluation (EME), Programme Grants for Applied Research. Applications via NIHR Research Management System. [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://www.nihr.ac.uk/funding-and-support/funding-for-research-studies/ → site root

sources.name: NIHR Global Health Research
sources.notes: Dedicated NIHR arm for LMIC health research. Funds Global Health Research Groups and Units. Highly relevant for LiGHT Lab / Rwanda AI Lab / ULTR-AI TB diagnostic work. [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://www.nihr.ac.uk/explore-nihr/programmes/global-health-research.htm → site root

sources.name: NHS AI Lab (NHS England)
sources.notes: NHS England AI accelerator; grants and implementation funding for clinical AI including AI Award rounds. Monitor for open calls.

sources.name: Cancer Research UK (CRUK)
sources.notes: One of the world's largest cancer research charities; >£450M annual research investment. Joint calls with American Cancer Society (US PI budget due June 11, 2026). Applications via ProposalCentral for some programmes.

sources.name: FCDO DevTracker (UK Foreign Aid)
sources.notes: Built on IATI OIPA API; data updated monthly; JSON download per programme.

sources.name: UK International Development Funding (GOV.UK)
sources.notes: No API; scraping required. Published under IATI standard.

sources.name: Leverhulme Trust
sources.notes: Major UK research funder across all subject areas except medical research. Full applications in new grants management system from autumn 2026.

sources.name: Nuffield Foundation
sources.notes: UK foundation funding research and student programmes to advance educational opportunity and social well-being. [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://www.nuffieldfoundation.org/funding/ → site root

sources.name: Joseph Rowntree Foundation
sources.notes: UK foundation working to unlock the UK from poverty. Open calls published at jrf.org.uk/funding.

sources.name: Esmée Fairbairn Foundation
sources.notes: One of the largest UK grant-makers. Open application process; apply via online portal. Data accessible via 360Giving.

sources.name: Gatsby Charitable Foundation
sources.notes: Active in plant science, neuroscience, education, Africa. Data accessible via 360Giving.

sources.name: Paul Hamlyn Foundation
sources.notes: [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://phf.org.uk/funds/ → site root

sources.name: IATI Datastore / D-Portal
sources.notes: REST API returning XML/JSON/CSV; d-portal.org is the browser-based explorer. [audit 2026-07-22] IATI Datastore now requires free subscription key — set IATI_API_KEY then unpause.

sources.name: AidData (College of William & Mary)
sources.notes: No live REST API; bulk dataset downloads in CSV/Stata format.

sources.name: World Bank Open Data / Projects
sources.notes: Comprehensive free API; Indicators API, Projects API, Data Catalog API all documented.

sources.name: Asian Development Bank (ADB) Tenders & Grants
sources.notes: No formal public API; RSS feeds available.

sources.name: Inter-American Development Bank (IDB/IADB)
sources.notes: No public API; scraping required.

sources.name: African Development Bank (AfDB) Tenders
sources.notes: No public API; scraping required.

sources.name: European Bank for Reconstruction and Development (EBRD – ECEPP)
sources.notes: No public API; ECEPP portal requires registration.

sources.name: European Investment Bank (EIB)
sources.notes: Open data portal with bulk downloads; no REST API but structured datasets available.

sources.name: UNDP Funding Windows Portal
sources.notes: No API; scraping required; UNDP publishes some IATI data.

sources.name: UN Global Marketplace (UNGM) – Procurement
sources.notes: No public API; scraping required.

sources.name: UNICEF Supply Division / Procurement
sources.notes: No API; scraping required. [audit 2026-07-22] auto-paused: http_404 and site root dead

sources.name: WHO Procurement / Calls
sources.notes: No API; scraping required.

sources.name: USAID (foreign aid grants/contracts)
sources.notes: Opportunities accessible via SAM.gov API and Grants.gov API. USAID DEC now archived.

sources.name: Global Fund to Fight AIDS, TB & Malaria
sources.notes: No API; limited structured data; scraping required. [audit 2026-07-22] redirects to https://resources.theglobalfund.org/en/ — verify new URL

sources.name: Gavi, the Vaccine Alliance
sources.notes: No API; scraping required. [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://www.gavi.org/programmes-impact/programmatic-policies/funding-opportunities → site root

sources.name: UNESCO – Grants & Prizes
sources.notes: UNESCO prizes, fellowships, and grants across all mandate areas. [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://www.unesco.org/en/prizes-grants → site root

sources.name: UN Women – Fund for Gender Equality
Google.org – Impact Challenges & Grants
sources.notes: No public API; monitor for open calls on google.org. Active Impact Challenges include AI for Science ($30M global), AI for Government Innovation ($1–3M per awardee). Digital Futures Fund >$35M committed globally.

sources.name: Google Health / Google DeepMind for Health
sources.notes: Separate from Google.org — direct DeepMind/Google Health research partnerships and grants. AI diagnostic imaging and LMIC health AI programmes.

sources.name: Microsoft AI for Good
sources.notes: Programs include AI for Health, AI for Earth, AI for Humanitarian Action, AI for Cultural Heritage, AI for Accessibility. Grants and Azure credits awarded to nonprofits and researchers.

sources.name: Meta AI for Social Good
sources.notes: Meta's research grants programme — fellowship and grant tracks for AI research. [audit 2026-07-22] auto-paused: http_other and site root dead

sources.name: AWS Diagnostic Development Initiative
sources.notes: AWS credits + cash grants for health AI diagnostics startups and researchers. Highly relevant for ULTR-AI TB POCUS AI work. [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://aws.amazon.com/health/diagnostic-development-initiative/ → site root

sources.name: Bezos Earth Fund
sources.notes: $10B committed to climate and nature. AI for Climate and Nature Grand Challenge: up to $100M total; Phase II awardees receive up to $2M each. [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://www.bezosearthfund.org/funding-opportunities → site root

sources.name: Schmidt Sciences (formerly Schmidt Futures)
sources.notes: $1.59B in assets; $228.7M in giving in FY2024. Active programs: AI2050 fellowships, carbon cycle research, HAVI institute. Rebranded from Schmidt Futures in 2024. Competitive RFPs at schmidtsciences.smapply.io.

sources.name: Skoll Foundation
sources.notes: Funds social entrepreneurs. Does not accept unsolicited proposals. Also in Candid FDO.

sources.name: Patrick J. McGovern Foundation
sources.notes: Boston-based; Dec 2024 announced $73.5M toward AI for Humanity supporting 144 organizations. One of 10 founding funders of the Humanity AI initiative ($500M over 5 years). No public API; monitor for open RFPs.

sources.name: Mozilla Foundation – Grants & Fellowships
sources.notes: Funds trustworthy AI, internet health, privacy, open technology. Founding funder of Humanity AI initiative. Active: Mozilla Technology Fund and Awards. [audit 2026-07-22] redirects to https://www.mozillafoundation.org/en/what-we-do/grantmaking/ — verify new URL

sources.name: Omidyar Network
sources.notes: Does not accept unsolicited grant proposals. Co-founder of Humanity AI initiative ($500M). Also in Candid FDO.

sources.name: Luminate (Omidyar spinoff)
sources.notes: Spinoff from Omidyar Network; data governance + democracy + civic technology focus.

sources.name: ClimateWorks Foundation
sources.notes: Since 2008 granted >$2B to 850+ grantees. Application by invitation only or selective calls — no open rolling applications.

sources.name: Digital Square (PATH)
sources.notes: Exclusively funds digital health implementation in LMICs; funded by USAID, Gates, and others. Active open calls for digital health goods. Highly relevant for MAM*AI and ULTR-AI deployment work. [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://digitalsquare.org/funding → site root

sources.name: EDCTP – European and Developing Countries Clinical Trials Partnership
sources.notes: €1.5B+ EU–Africa partnership for clinical research on poverty-related diseases. EDCTP3 / Global Health EDCTP3 active. Directly relevant for ULTR-AI TB diagnostics and WITS partnership.

sources.name: AUDA-NEPAD – African Union Development Agency
sources.notes: Implements AU flagship projects; direct Africa-led AI and health funding. Highly relevant for Rwanda National Health AI Laboratory. [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://www.nepad.org/funding-opportunities → site root

sources.name: Africa CDC Innovation Fund
sources.notes: Emerging funder for African digital health and AI surveillance systems. Aligned with Rwanda AI Lab mandate.

sources.name: Rwanda ICT / AI Innovation Fund (RISA)
sources.notes: Rwanda Information Society Authority; direct government partner for Rwanda National Health AI Laboratory. Government grants and co-investment for AI deployment in Rwanda. [audit 2026-07-22] auto-paused: http_404 and site root dead

sources.name: Tony Elumelu Foundation
sources.notes: Africa's leading entrepreneurship philanthropy. $100M TEF programme. Health tech increasingly in scope.

sources.name: GIZ – Deutsche Gesellschaft für Internationale Zusammenarbeit
sources.notes: German federal development agency; significant AI/health project funding for LMICs. Active digital health and AI calls for sub-Saharan Africa. [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://www.giz.de/en/html/financing_opportunities.html → site root

sources.name: AFD – French Development Agency (Agence française de développement)
sources.notes: Funds health, climate, digital infrastructure in francophone Africa and globally. FSP (Solidarity Fund for Priority Projects) open calls. [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://www.afd.fr/en/calls-for-proposals → site root

sources.name: Co-IMPACT
sources.notes: Collaborative funder (Gates, USAID, Mastercard etc.) for social systems change in Africa and South Asia.

sources.name: FIND – Foundation for Innovative New Diagnostics
sources.notes: Global diagnostics nonprofit; TB/HIV/malaria AI diagnostic development calls highly relevant for ULTR-AI POCUS AI work. Co-funded by Gates, Wellcome, USAID. [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://www.finddx.org/funding/ → site root

sources.name: Laerdal Foundation for Acute Medicine
sources.notes: Directly relevant for MAM*AI POCUS intrapartum surveillance and blind-sweep ultrasound work. Funds innovation in acute medicine access in LMICs.

sources.name: Wellcome–DBT India Alliance
sources.notes: India's major biomedical funder joint Wellcome + DBT. Open calls for Intermediate and Senior Fellowships for researchers in India. Highly relevant for Ashoka University partnership. [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://wellcomedbt.org/fellowships/ → site root

sources.name: ICRC Technology for Humanity
sources.notes: Direct funder of humanitarian AI projects; ASPECT collaboration with ICRC already in place. Monitor for open calls on humanitarian diffusion model and HARMONY-adjacent projects. [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://www.icrc.org/en/digital-transformation/humanitarian-technology → site root

sources.name: Ashoka Foundation – Fellowship & Grants
sources.notes: Social entrepreneurship fellowships. Directly relevant given LiGHT Lab Ashoka University partnership. Monitor for open nomination cycles.

sources.name: Aga Khan Foundation
sources.notes: Part of AKDN. Active in health, education, rural development. International Scholarship Programme open 2026–2027. No public REST API.

sources.name: CIHR – Canadian Institutes of Health Research
sources.notes: Canada's primary health research funder. Part of Canadian Tri-Agency (CIHR, NSERC, SSHRC). Applications via ResearchNet.

sources.name: NSERC – Natural Sciences and Engineering Research Council of Canada
sources.notes: Canada's federal funding agency for natural sciences and engineering. Part of Tri-Agency with CIHR and SSHRC. [audit 2026-07-22] redirects to https://nserc-crsng.canada.ca/en — verify new URL

sources.name: IDRC – International Development Research Centre (Canada)
sources.notes: Canadian Crown corporation funding applied research in developing countries. Partners with NRF South Africa, African Academy of Sciences, and others.

sources.name: SSHRC – Social Sciences and Humanities Research Council of Canada
sources.notes: Canada's federal funding agency for social sciences and humanities. Part of Tri-Agency. Key 2026: Connection Grants, Insight Grants, Partnership Grants.

sources.name: CFI – Canada Foundation for Innovation
sources.notes: Canadian Crown corporation investing in research facilities and equipment. In March 2026, Government of Canada invested >$552M via CFI.

sources.name: ARC – Australian Research Council
sources.notes: Australia's primary competitive research funder (excluding health/medical). Uses RMS for grant submission. [audit 2026-07-22] timeout during probe

sources.name: NHMRC – National Health and Medical Research Council (Australia)
sources.notes: Australia's primary health and medical research funder. Applications via Sapphire portal. Active 2026 joint call with SNSF for AI in healthcare research (deadline May 28, 2026). [audit 2026-07-22] timeout during probe

sources.name: CSIRO – Commonwealth Scientific and Industrial Research Organisation
sources.notes: Australia's national science agency; AI in health and data science calls. Relevant for potential EPFL/CSIRO collaborations. [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://www.csiro.au/en/work-with-us/funding-and-grants → site root

sources.name: NRF – National Research Foundation (South Africa)
sources.notes: South Africa's primary research funding agency. Partners with IDRC Canada, African Academy of Sciences through Science Granting Councils Initiative (SGCI). [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://www.nrf.ac.za/funding-opportunities/ → site root

sources.name: Wellcome–DBT India Alliance (Intermediate/Senior)
sources.notes: India's major biomedical funder; joint Wellcome + DBT. Open calls for researchers based in India. Relevant for Ashoka University partnership.

sources.name: DST-SERB – Science and Engineering Research Board (India)
sources.notes: India national science funder; CRG, MATRICS, TARE, SRG grant programmes. Relevant for Ashoka University collaboration.

sources.name: BIRAC – Biotechnology Industry Research Assistance Council (India)
sources.notes: India biotech innovation under DBT; BIPP, BIG, PACE programmes. AI diagnostics in LMICs angle.

sources.name: AMED – Japan Agency for Medical Research and Development
sources.notes: Japan's dedicated health R&D funder (~$1.3B/yr). AI diagnostics and global health calls. Active bilateral programmes.

sources.name: JSPS – Japan Society for the Promotion of Science (KAKENHI)
sources.notes: Japan's leading research funding agency; KAKENHI is Japan's primary competitive grant programme. Applications via e-Rad. Annual cycle: applications open July, close September.

sources.name: NRF – National Research Foundation of Korea
sources.notes: South Korea's primary research funding body. Active bilateral with JSPS Japan (2026 call active), NRF South Africa. [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://www.nrf.re.kr/eng/main → site root

sources.name: Singapore National Research Foundation (NRF-SG)
sources.notes: Major AI + health funding in Southeast Asia; RIE2025 programmes.

sources.name: NMRC – National Medical Research Council (Singapore)
sources.notes: Singapore health AI and clinical research funder. [audit 2026-07-22] auto-paused: dns_fail

sources.name: DBT – Department of Biotechnology (India)
sources.notes: India's federal biotechnology research funder. Also see BIRAC for industry grants and DST (dstindia.gov.in) for broader science grants. [audit 2026-07-22] timeout during probe

sources.name: Mo Ibrahim Foundation
sources.notes: Governance and leadership grants for Africa; AI governance angle. Ibrahim Prize and Fellowship programmes.

sources.name: Grand Challenges Canada
sources.notes: Canadian part of the Grand Challenges network; Saving Brains, Stars in Global Health, and Transition to Scale programmes. Highly relevant for MAM*AI and ULTR-AI.

sources.name: JICA – Japan International Cooperation Agency
sources.notes: Japanese ODA; digital health technology grants for LMICs including Africa. [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://www.jica.go.jp/english/our_work/types_of_assistance/grant/ → site root

sources.name: KOICA – Korea International Cooperation Agency
sources.notes: Korean ODA; digital health + AI in development context.

sources.name: CIFAR – Canadian Institute for Advanced Research
sources.notes: Global research network; AI + Health Fellows programme. Pan-Canadian AI Strategy funder. Supports researchers at institutions worldwide. [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://cifar.ca/research/apply-for-funding/ → site root

sources.name: Horizon Europe NCP Portal
sources.notes: No API; informational portal; links back to EU F&T Portal.

sources.name: Wellcome Leap
sources.notes: No API; small number of active calls; monitor directly. NOTE: Primary entry now listed under Wellcome Trust category with full detail.

sources.name: EUREKA Network (collaborative R&D)
sources.notes: No API; scraping required.

sources.name: Newton Fund / British Council
sources.notes: No API; scraping required. [audit 2026-07-22] timeout during probe

sources.name: GEF (Global Environment Facility) Projects
sources.notes: Open data portal with API; project browser and bulk data downloads.

sources.name: Green Climate Fund (GCF)
sources.notes: Open data portal; project data downloadable; REST API available.

sources.name: Adaptation Fund
sources.notes: No formal API; project list scrapeable.

sources.name: Schmidt Science Fellows
sources.notes: Elite postdoctoral fellowship for scientists pivoting across disciplines; AI + health. Run in partnership with Rhodes Trust.

sources.name: Fogarty International Center – NIH Global Health Fellowships
sources.notes: NIH global health research training grants; Africa AI programmes highly relevant for LiGHT Lab and Rwanda AI Lab.

sources.name: Wellcome Early Career Awards
sources.notes: Standalone Wellcome track distinct from main research schemes. For early-career researchers in biomedical and health sciences.

sources.name: Branco Weiss Fellowship (ETH Zurich / EPFL-adjacent)
sources.notes: Society in Science fellowship for unconventional postdoctoral research at any institution worldwide. Directly EPFL-adjacent given LiGHT Lab location. Highly prestigious.

sources.name: Guggenheim Fellowship
sources.notes: Prestigious annual fellowships for scholars, artists, scientists. [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://www.gf.org/applicants/ → site root

sources.name: ACLS – Fellowships & Grants
sources.notes: American Council of Learned Societies — major humanities fellowships.

sources.name: Fulbright Program – US Scholar
sources.notes: US government flagship international exchange program. [audit 2026-07-22] redirects to https://fulbrightscholars.org/what-fulbright/opportunities — verify new URL

sources.name: DAAD – German Academic Exchange Scholarships
sources.notes: German government academic exchange — hundreds of programs.

sources.name: Chevening Scholarships
sources.notes: UK government scholarships for future leaders. [audit 2026-07-22] timeout during probe

sources.name: NWO Veni/Vidi/Vici Talent Programme (Netherlands)
sources.notes: Dutch career development scheme; open to international researchers at Dutch institutions.

sources.name: American Academy in Rome – Rome Prize
sources.notes: 11-month residential fellowships in Rome.

sources.name: MacArthur Foundation – Fellowships ('Genius Grant')
sources.notes: No-strings 'genius grant' — $800K over 5 years. By nomination only.

sources.name: Swiss Government Excellence Scholarships
sources.notes: Swiss federal scholarships for foreign researchers and artists. EPFL-adjacent.

sources.name: Whiting Foundation – Grants & Fellowships
sources.notes: [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://www.whiting.org/writers/whiting-award → site root

sources.name: Pulitzer Center – Grants
sources.notes: [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://pulitzercenter.org/grants-and-fellowships → site root

sources.name: Thomas J. Watson Fellowship
sources.notes: One year of independent exploration abroad for graduating seniors.

sources.name: Nieman Foundation – Fellowships
sources.notes: Harvard journalism fellowships.

sources.name: Heinrich Böll Stiftung – Scholarships
sources.notes: German Green foundation — international scholarships and grants. [audit 2026-07-22] auto-paused: http_other and site root dead

sources.name: Friedrich Ebert Stiftung – Scholarships
sources.notes: German Social Democrat foundation scholarships.

sources.name: Alzheimer's Association – International Research Grant Program
sources.notes: Funds research on Alzheimer's. Open to US and international researchers. Applications via Grants.gov or foundation portal.

sources.name: American Cancer Society – Research Grants
sources.notes: Electronic application deadlines June 1 and December 1. Application via ProposalCentral. Joint calls with Cancer Research UK.

sources.name: American Heart Association – Research Funding
sources.notes: Applications submitted via ProposalCentral (research.americanheart.org).

sources.name: JDRF – Juvenile Diabetes Research Foundation
sources.notes: International nonprofit for T1D research. Also accessible via Candid FDO. [audit 2026-07-22] redirects to https://www.breakthrought1d.org/explore-research/ — verify new URL

sources.name: Cystic Fibrosis Foundation – Research Grants
sources.notes: Applications via awards.cff.org portal. Two annual cycles (spring and fall).

sources.name: British Heart Foundation
sources.notes: Major UK funder for cardiovascular science. Applications via BHF portal and ProposalCentral for some grants. [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://www.bhf.org.uk/for-professionals/information-for-researchers/apply-for-a-grant → site root

sources.name: Novartis Foundation
sources.notes: Swiss pharma foundation; tropical disease + health AI in LMICs. Selective grant programme.

sources.name: Johnson & Johnson Foundation – Global Health
sources.notes: Global health access + health worker AI training. Relevant for MAM*AI health worker capacity building angle. [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://www.jnjmedtech.com/en-US/our-commitment/global-health → site root

sources.name: Roche Foundation
sources.notes: Diagnostics + access to healthcare; AI-based diagnostic calls relevant for ULTR-AI. [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://www.roche.com/sustainability/philanthropy/roche-foundation → site root

sources.name: Sanofi Espoir Foundation
sources.notes: Sub-Saharan Africa health + malnutrition access; MAM*AI angle for acute malnutrition and maternal care. [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://www.sanofi.com/en/our-responsibility/communities/the-sanofi-espoir-foundation → site root

sources.name: Philips Foundation – Health Access
sources.notes: Funds health access innovations in LMICs including digital diagnostics. Relevant for POCUS AI work.

sources.name: ProposalCentral – Grant Submission Aggregator
sources.notes: E-grantmaking platform hosting grant opportunities for hundreds of organizations including American Heart Association, American Cancer Society, Cystic Fibrosis Foundation, AACR, PhRMA, HDSA. Lists all current open opportunities at proposalcentral.com/GrantOpportunities.asp.

sources.name: DevelopmentAid.org
sources.notes: Paid API for partners; automates retrieval of grants, tenders, jobs.

sources.name: Candid Foundation Directory Online (FDO)
sources.notes: Grants API with /funders and /recipients endpoints. Subscription-based.

sources.name: Candid / 990 Finder (Nonprofit data)
sources.notes: Multiple APIs: Grants, Essentials, Profiles. Tiered pricing.

sources.name: Instrumentl
sources.notes: No public API; closed SaaS platform. Subscription required for access.

sources.name: GrantWatch
sources.notes: No API; $249/yr subscription; scraping possible but ToS may restrict.

sources.name: Pivot-RP (ProQuest/Clarivate)
sources.notes: API used by universities for internal matching tools; requires institutional subscription.

sources.name: OpenGrants.io
sources.notes: REST/JSON API; developer portal at developer.opengrants.io; invite-only access.

sources.name: GrantStation
sources.notes: No public API; membership subscription required.

sources.name: fundsforNGOs
sources.notes: No API; free website; scraping feasible.

sources.name: Terra Viva Grants
sources.notes: No API; free listings; scraping feasible.

sources.name: 360Giving GrantNav (UK Philanthropic Grants)
sources.notes: Free HTTP/JSON API; bulk CSV/JSON download of full dataset; Datastore access for complex queries. [audit 2026-07-22] GrantNav API discontinued (404 on /api/*) — needs new integration via 360Giving data downloads.

sources.name: OpenAlex (research funding metadata)
sources.notes: Free open API; no key required for polite pool (<10 req/sec); CC0 data.

sources.name: ProPublica Nonprofit Explorer (IRS 990)
sources.notes: Free API; follows simple data terms of use; v2 API active.

sources.name: OpenOpps.com (World Bank, ADB, IDB tenders)
sources.notes: Commercial API aggregating multilateral development bank procurement.

sources.name: SPIN – Sponsored Programs Information Network (InfoEd)
sources.notes: Grant database with 8,000+ current funding opportunities from 10,000+ sponsors. Updated almost daily. Provided via institutional subscriptions. Used by Yale, Penn, UNC.

sources.name: GrantForward
sources.notes: Subscription-based funding opportunity aggregator. 30,000+ sponsors. Formerly GrantFinder.

sources.name: Inside Philanthropy (Grant Finder)
sources.notes: No API; subscription required; scraping restricted.

sources.name: ResearchProfessional (Clarivate)
sources.notes: Institutional API available; subscription required.

sources.name: Dimensions (Digital Science) – Grants
sources.notes: DSL query language API; free tier for non-commercial; institutional subscription for full access. [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://www.dimensions.ai/products/grants/ → site root

sources.name: CrossRef Grant Metadata
sources.notes: Free REST API; funder data underpins OpenAlex and Dimensions grant metadata.

sources.name: Europe PubMed Central (Europe PMC) – Grants
sources.notes: REST API with grant search; supports funders who mandate open access.

sources.name: Semantic Scholar (AI2) – Funding metadata
sources.notes: Free API key; paper funding data extracted by AI; less complete than Dimensions.

sources.name: UK AI Safety Institute / DSIT
sources.notes: UK gov AI research funding post-Frontier AI Safety Summit; clinical AI evaluation and safety focus. Monitor for open calls.

sources.name: French AI National Strategy (Programme IA / ANR IA)
sources.notes: French sovereign AI grants; health AI innovation separate from ANR main programmes. Also see ANR IA targeted calls.

sources.name: Pro Helvetia – Swiss Arts Council
sources.notes: Swiss federal foundation supporting artists and cultural projects internationally.

sources.name: Mondriaan Fund – Dutch Arts
sources.notes: Netherlands arts funder — visual art, design, architecture grants.

sources.name: Prince Claus Fund
sources.notes: Supports artists in contexts where cultural freedom is restricted. [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://www.princeclausfund.org/programmes → site root

sources.name: Arts Council England – Grants
sources.notes: National arts funder for England.

sources.name: Ars Electronica – Open Calls
sources.notes: Relevant for Ephemorphic and ICRC humanitarian diffusion model art projects. [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://www.ars.electronica.art/news/calls/ → site root

sources.name: e-flux – Open Calls
sources.notes: Major art world announcements hub — open calls, grants, residencies.

sources.name: Eyebeam – Residency & Grants
sources.notes: [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://www.eyebeam.org/residency/ → site root

sources.name: Creative Capital
sources.notes: US artist support org — multi-year financial and advisory support.

sources.name: United States Artists – Fellowships
sources.notes: $50K unrestricted fellowships for US artists. [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://www.unitedstatesartists.org/usa-fellows/ → site root

sources.name: Sundance Institute – Grants
sources.notes: Supports independent storytellers in film, theatre, digital. [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://www.sundance.org/programs/artist-programs → site root

sources.name: Res Artis – Worldwide Residency Network
sources.notes: Global network of 700+ artist residencies — listing open calls. [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://www.resartis.org/opportunities/ → site root

sources.notes: [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://www.culturalfoundation.eu/grants → site root

sources.name: NSF Award Search
sources.notes: PAUSED: NSF Award Search returns past awards, not open solicitations. Use NSF Open Solicitations (Grants.gov) for funding calls. Award registry may be repurposed for prior-art search later.

sources.name: New England Foundation for the Arts
Nordic Culture Fund
sources.notes: Supports Nordic cultural cooperation.

sources.name: Nuffield Foundation – Grants
sources.notes: Secondary URL entry. See 'Nuffield Foundation' under UK Private Foundation.

sources.name: Open Philanthropy (formerly OpenPhil)
sources.notes: No API; structured HTML — relatively easy to scrape. Note: canonical entry is 'Open Philanthropy' — this entry retained for legacy name compatibility. [audit 2026-07-22] redirects to https://coefficientgiving.org/funds/ — verify new URL

sources.name: Open Society Foundations – Fellowships
sources.notes: Soros-backed foundation — major international grants. See also 'Open Society Foundations' under Major Private Foundation.

sources.name: Paul Hamlyn Foundation – Grants
sources.notes: See also 'Paul Hamlyn Foundation' under UK Private Foundation. [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://phf.org.uk/funds/ → site root

sources.name: Pew Charitable Trusts – Grants
sources.notes: Secondary URL entry. See 'Pew Charitable Trusts' under Major Private Foundation. [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://www.pewtrusts.org/en/grants → site root

sources.name: Porticus Foundation – Grants
sources.notes: Brenninkmeijer family foundation — global grantmaker.

sources.name: Robert Bosch Stiftung – Grants
sources.notes: Arts/culture programmes entry. See also 'Robert Bosch Stiftung (Robert Bosch Foundation)' under EU / European for full entry. [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://www.bosch-stiftung.de/en/projects-and-programmes → site root

sources.name: Rosa Luxemburg Stiftung – Scholarships
sources.notes: [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://www.rosalux.de/en/scholarships → site root

sources.name: Schmidt Futures – Grants
sources.notes: Legacy URL (schmidtfutures.com). Organisation rebranded to Schmidt Sciences in 2024. See 'Schmidt Sciences (formerly Schmidt Futures)' for current entry. [audit 2026-07-22] redirects to https://www.schmidtsciences.org/ — verify new URL

sources.name: TransArtists – Residency Database
sources.notes: Dutch-based global residency database.

sources.name: Tribeca Foundation – Grants
sources.notes: [audit 2026-07-22] listing path http_404; repointed https://www.tribecafilm.com/foundation → site root

sources.name: Volkswagen Stiftung – Funding
sources.notes: Secondary URL entry. See 'VolkswagenStiftung (Volkswagen Foundation)' under EU / European for primary entry.

sources.name: Wellcome Leap (health moonshots)
sources.notes: Legacy entry name. See 'Wellcome Leap – Research Challenges' and 'Wellcome Leap' under Major Private Foundation / International for primary entries.

sources.name: Wellcome Open Research (publications/grants)
sources.notes: F1000-based developer API for article/grant data.

sources.name: Wellcome Trust – Open Calls
sources.notes: Secondary entry targeting wellcome.org/grant-funding. See 'Wellcome Trust' under Major Private Foundation for primary entry.

sources.name: d-portal (IATI visualizer)
sources.notes: No separate API; access data via IATI Datastore API. [audit 2026-07-22] IATI Datastore now requires free subscription key — set IATI_API_KEY then unpause.

sources.name: eCivis (Government Grant Management)
sources.notes: API for enterprise clients; no public documentation. [audit 2026-07-22] redirects to https://eunasolutions.com/solutions/grants/?utm_source=redirect&utm_medium=referral&utm_content=ecivis — verify new URL
